content.subject: Task 1: Extend OpenSessionInput with proxyConfig
content.description: Add ProxyConfig type + optional proxyConfig field to OpenSessionInput in chat-orchestrator AND mirror it on sandbox-subprocess. Test stub captures field. it.todo until Task 2.

content.subject: Task 2: Wire proxy:open-session/close-session into orchestrator
content.description: Orchestrator runtime-checks bus.hasService('proxy:open-session'), opens before sandbox, threads proxyConfig, closes in finally. Translates tcp:// or unix:// endpoint shape.

content.subject: Task 3: Sandbox-subprocess writes CA + injects proxy env
content.description: When proxyConfig present, write CA to per-session tmpfile and inject HTTPS_PROXY/HTTP_PROXY/NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS/SSL_CERT_FILE/AX_PROXY_ENDPOINT/AX_PROXY_UNIX_SOCKET + envMap.
content.activeForm: Wiring CA + proxy env in sandbox-subprocess

content.subject: Task 4: SDK runner — bridge startup + drop ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL
content.description: Extend RunnerEnv with proxyEndpoint/proxyUnixSocket; AX_LLM_PROXY_URL becomes optional XOR. Start bridge if AX_PROXY_UNIX_SOCKET; rewrite HTTP(S)_PROXY in-process. Skip ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL when proxy wired.

content.subject: Task 5: Wire credential-proxy into CLI + extend dev-agents-stub
content.description: CLI loads createCredentialProxyPlugin (TCP listen). Stub returns allowedHosts: ['api.anthropic.com'] + requiredCredentials.

content.subject: Task 6: Wire credential-proxy into k8s preset
content.description: Same wiring in presets/k8s, Unix socket variant. Update preset.test.ts plugin-name list.

content.subject: Task 7: Audit-log subscribes to event.http-egress (verify or add)
content.description: Verify audit-log already subscribes; if not, add subscription that writes one line per egress.

content.subject: Task 8: E2E test against real Anthropic API (gated)
content.description: Add AX_TEST_ANTHROPIC_KEY-gated test that round-trips a chat through the proxy with real Anthropic API.

content.subject: Task 9: PR notes — boundary review + half-wired window closure
content.description: Add docs/plans/2026-04-28-phase-2-pr-notes.md with boundary review block + Phase 1a half-wired window closure.

content.subject: Task 10: Push branch + open PR + watch CI
content.description: Push phase-2-runner-proxy-wiring; open PR with full body; gh pr checks --watch.

content.description: Add ProxyConfig type + optional proxyConfig field to OpenSessionInput in chat-orchestrator AND mirror it on sandbox-subprocess. Test stub captures field. it.todo until Task 2.

content.subject: Task 2: Wire proxy:open-session/close-session into orchestrator
content.description: Orchestrator runtime-checks bus.hasService('proxy:open-session'), opens before sandbox, threads proxyConfig, closes in finally. Translates tcp:// or unix:// endpoint shape.

content.subject: Task 3: Sandbox-subprocess writes CA + injects proxy env
content.description: When proxyConfig present, write CA to per-session tmpfile and inject HTTPS_PROXY/HTTP_PROXY/NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS/SSL_CERT_FILE/AX_PROXY_ENDPOINT/AX_PROXY_UNIX_SOCKET + envMap.
content.activeForm: Wiring CA + proxy env in sandbox-subprocess

content.subject: Task 4: SDK runner — bridge startup + drop ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL
content.description: Extend RunnerEnv with proxyEndpoint/proxyUnixSocket; AX_LLM_PROXY_URL becomes optional XOR. Start bridge if AX_PROXY_UNIX_SOCKET; rewrite HTTP(S)_PROXY in-process. Skip ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL when proxy wired.

content.subject: Task 5: Wire credential-proxy into CLI + extend dev-agents-stub
content.description: CLI loads createCredentialProxyPlugin (TCP listen). Stub returns allowedHosts: ['api.anthropic.com'] + requiredCredentials.

content.subject: Task 6: Wire credential-proxy into k8s preset
content.description: Same wiring in presets/k8s, Unix socket variant. Update preset.test.ts plugin-name list.

content.subject: Task 7: Audit-log subscribes to event.http-egress (verify or add)
content.description: Verify audit-log already subscribes; if not, add subscription that writes one line per egress.

content.subject: Task 8: E2E test against real Anthropic API (gated)
content.description: Add AX_TEST_ANTHROPIC_KEY-gated test that round-trips a chat through the proxy with real Anthropic API.

content.subject: Task 9: PR notes — boundary review + half-wired window closure
content.description: Add docs/plans/2026-04-28-phase-2-pr-notes.md with boundary review block + Phase 1a half-wired window closure.

content.subject: Task 10: Push branch + open PR + watch CI
content.description: Push phase-2-runner-proxy-wiring; open PR with full body; gh pr checks --watch.

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(preset-k8s): load @ax/credential-proxy on Unix socket (Phase 2)

The k8s preset now loads @ax/credential-proxy on
/var/run/ax/proxy.sock (overridable via the new optional
credentialProxy.socketPath / caDir config fields, also readable from
the AX_PROXY_SOCKET_PATH env var). Helm chart wires the matching
emptyDir mount on host pods; sandbox pods get the SAME emptyDir so
they can dial the socket. The runner-side @ax/credential-proxy-bridge
converts that to a loopback TCP port inside the sandbox.

Both acceptance.test.ts and multi-tenant-acceptance.test.ts now pass
per-test tmpdir overrides for socketPath + caDir — unprivileged test
users can't bind /var/run/ax and we don't want test cruft in the
user's home.

The plugin-name list in preset.test.ts grows '@ax/credential-proxy';
the next refactor that drops a plugin without updating that list will
trip the canary.

Real Anthropic credentials still need to be admin-seeded (Phase 9.5
extends @ax/agents with per-row allowedHosts/requiredCredentials);
in the meantime production deploys must seed an agent record with
those fields populated for the canary to round-trip.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

feat(audit-log): subscribe to event.http-egress (Phase 2)

The audit-log plugin now writes one storage row per egress, keyed by
egress:<sessionId>:<timestamp>. allowlist-miss events that lack a
session (no per-session config matched) land under egress:unscoped:*
so the row still persists for forensic reconstruction.

Pass-through subscriber: the proxy already decided whether to block
at request time and stamped blockedReason; audit-log is observation,
not policy.

The HttpEgressEvent shape is duplicated structurally per I2 — no
import from @ax/credential-proxy. Field drift surfaces as a runtime
shape error at fire time, which is the v2-wide contract for hook
shapes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

the real Anthropic key satisfies the API, and the proxy is the only
component that holds it.

content.content:
# Phase 1a — PR notes

**Branch:** `feat/phase-1a-credential-proxy`
**Plan:** `docs/plans/2026-04-27-phase-1a-credential-proxy-impl.md`
**Design doc:** `docs/plans/2026-04-27-agent-centric-simplification-design.md` Section 1 + Section 2
**Security note:** `docs/plans/2026-04-27-phase-1a-security-note.md`

## What lands

Two new additive packages, ~1100 LOC ported from v1's `~/dev/ai/ax/src/host/`:

| Package | Role | v1 source |
|---|---|---|
| `@ax/credential-proxy` | Host-side MITM proxy: TLS termination + credential substitution + canary scan + private-IP block + DNS rebinding defense + per-session allowlist gate | `web-proxy.ts` (656 LOC) + `proxy-ca.ts` (124 LOC) + `credential-placeholders.ts` (122 LOC) |
| `@ax/credential-proxy-bridge` | Sandbox-side library: TCP↔Unix-socket relay so k8s pods can reach the host's proxy | `web-proxy-bridge.ts` (174 LOC) |

Both packages exercised end-to-end by an integration test: bridge → unix socket → proxy → MITM → mock LLM upstream. Substituted credential arrives at upstream; subscriber receives one `event.http-egress` with `classification: 'llm'`.

## What does NOT land (deferred per design Section 7)

- **No wiring into `cli/main.ts`** — the plugin is loaded only by its own tests this phase. **Phase 2** wires it into `agent-claude-sdk-runner` (~1 week).
- **No changes to `@ax/credentials`** — the existing `({id}) → {value}` hook shape stays. **Phase 1b** splits the plugin into facade + storage backend + per-kind resolvers and reshapes the hook contract.
- **No OAuth lifecycle** — Phase 3.

## Half-wired note (CLAUDE.md tension flagged in plan)

CLAUDE.md says *"either fully registered + tested + reachable from the canary acceptance test, or it doesn't merge."* Phase 1a is integration-tested but not loaded by the production CLI. Trade-off accepted per design Section 7 (`Risk: Low. New plugins, not loaded anywhere yet.`); Phase 2 closes the loop within ~1 week.

If Phase 2 stalls beyond a week, this PR turns into the trap the policy was written to prevent. Track explicitly.

## Boundary review

New service hooks: `proxy:open-session`, `proxy:rotate-session`, `proxy:close-session`.

- **Alternate impl this hook could have:** a different proxy backend — e.g., a per-pod sidecar instead of the shared-host listener that v2 ships, or a Cloud-NAT-based gateway with a real cert. Same hook surface, different transport + cert authority.
- **Payload field names that might leak:** `proxyEndpoint` (opaque URI string — `tcp://...` or `unix://...`), `caCertPem` (PEM string), `envMap` (`Record<string, string>` of placeholder values). None leak backend specifics.
- **Subscriber risk:** these are service hooks (one impl), so no subscriber risk on the `proxy:*` surface. The `event.http-egress` subscriber payload uses HTTP-generic fields (`host`, `path`, `status`, `requestBytes`, `responseBytes`, `durationMs`, `credentialInjected`, `classification`, `blockedReason`, `timestamp`) — nothing k8s-specific or backend-specific.
- **Wire surface:** none of these are IPC actions. Internal host hooks only.

## Security review

(See `docs/plans/2026-04-27-phase-1a-security-note.md` for the full walk.)

```
## Security review
- Sandbox: New host plugin tightens sandbox egress to per-session allowlist + private-IP block (incl. IPv4-mapped IPv6 unwrap, Azure IMDS, CGNAT). Real credentials never leave the host process — sandbox env contains placeholders only. Reduces capability vs. existing baseline.
- Injection: Untrusted bytes from sandbox flow through `SharedCredentialRegistry.replaceAllBuffer` (string-replace, no eval). Response path NEVER substitutes (I7 — verified at `listener.ts:542`). Audit event payload tags untrusted strings as data.
- Supply chain: Added `node-forge@^1.3.1` (X.509 cert ops, ~3M dl/wk, no install scripts) and `undici@^7` (already transitive; bridge promotes to direct dep). Caret pinning per project convention; tighter pins for credential-proxy a worthwhile follow-up.
```

## Invariants verified (from impl plan)

| | What | Where verified |
|---|---|---|
| I1 | Real credentials never leave host process | `acceptance.test.ts` — placeholder in sandbox env, real value at upstream |
| I2 | Allowlist is the only egress gate | `listener-http.test.ts`, `listener-connect-bypass.test.ts` — 403 on disallowed host |
| I3 | Private IP ranges blocked (incl. IPv4-mapped IPv6, Azure IMDS, CGNAT) | `private-ip.test.ts` (46 tests across CIDR ranges) |
| I4 | Canary scan aborts before forward + emits `blockedReason: 'canary'` | `listener-connect-mitm.test.ts` — upstream never receives, event captured |
| I5 | CA private key mode 0600 | `ca.test.ts` — `statSync(...).mode & 0o777 === 0o600` |
| I6 | Placeholders are 16 random bytes | `registry.test.ts` — regex `/^ax-cred:[0-9a-f]{32}$/` |
| I7 | No substitution on response path | `listener.ts:542` (response-side `targetTls.on('data')` writes without `replaceAllBuffer`) |
| I8 | Bridge contains zero policy | `bridge.ts` — no allowlist, no IP check, no substitution; pure relay |
| I9 | Plugin additive (no consumer wiring) | `cli/main.ts` untouched; integration test is the consumer |

## Stats

- 26 commits on the branch
- ~1100 LOC of v1 helpers ported into 2 new packages + supporting tests
- 76 new tests (67 in credential-proxy, 8 in credential-proxy-bridge, 1 integration)
- Total repo tests: 1607 → 1683 (no regressions)
- 30 files changed (all scoped to `packages/credential-proxy/`, `packages/credential-proxy-bridge/`, `docs/plans/`, `pnpm-lock.yaml`, `tsconfig.json`)
- `pnpm build` clean (root references include both new packages)
- `pnpm lint` clean
- `pnpm test` clean

## Follow-ups (don't block this PR)

- **Phase 2** — wire proxy + bridge into `agent-claude-sdk-runner`. Closes the half-wired window.
- **Tighter pinning** for `node-forge` (exact version vs caret) — credential-proxy is security-critical.
- **Corrupt-CA-on-disk recovery** — currently the CA-load path doesn't validate; a malformed PEM throws on first `forge.pki.privateKeyFromPem` call. v1 has the same shape.

| | What | Where verified |
|---|---|---|
| I1 | Real credentials never leave host process | `acceptance.test.ts` — placeholder in sandbox env, real value at upstream |
| I2 | Allowlist is the only egress gate | `listener-http.test.ts`, `listener-connect-bypass.test.ts` — 403 on disallowed host |
| I3 | Private IP ranges blocked (incl. IPv4-mapped IPv6, Azure IMDS, CGNAT) | `private-ip.test.ts` (46 tests across CIDR ranges) |
| I4 | Canary scan aborts before forward + emits `blockedReason: 'canary'` | `listener-connect-mitm.test.ts` — upstream never receives, event captured |
| I5 | CA private key mode 0600 | `ca.test.ts` — `statSync(...).mode & 0o777 === 0o600` |
| I6 | Placeholders are 16 random bytes | `registry.test.ts` — regex `/^ax-cred:[0-9a-f]{32}$/` |
| I7 | No substitution on response path | `listener.ts:542` (response-side `targetTls.on('data')` writes without `replaceAllBuffer`) |
| I8 | Bridge contains zero policy | `bridge.ts` — no allowlist, no IP check, no substitution; pure relay |
| I9 | Plugin additive (no consumer wiring) | `cli/main.ts` untouched; integration test is the consumer |

## Stats

- 26 commits on the branch
- ~1100 LOC of v1 helpers ported into 2 new packages + supporting tests
- 76 new tests (67 in credential-proxy, 8 in credential-proxy-bridge, 1 integration)
- Total repo tests: 1607 → 1683 (no regressions)
- 30 files changed (all scoped to `packages/credential-proxy/`, `packages/credential-proxy-bridge/`, `docs/plans/`, `pnpm-lock.yaml`, `tsconfig.json`)
- `pnpm build` clean (root references include both new packages)
- `pnpm lint` clean
- `pnpm test` clean

## Follow-ups (don't block this PR)

- **Phase 2** — wire proxy + bridge into `agent-claude-sdk-runner`. Closes the half-wired window.
- **Tighter pinning** for `node-forge` (exact version vs caret) — credential-proxy is security-critical.
- **Corrupt-CA-on-disk recovery** — currently the CA-load path doesn't validate; a malformed PEM throws on first `forge.pki.privateKeyFromPem` call. v1 has the same shape.

input.content:
# Phase 2 — PR notes

**Branch:** `phase-2-runner-proxy-wiring`
**Plan:** `docs/plans/2026-04-28-phase-2-runner-proxy-wiring-impl.md`
**Design doc:** `docs/plans/2026-04-27-agent-centric-simplification-design.md` Section 1 + Section 5
**Predecessor:** Phase 1a (PR #1) — credential-proxy + bridge shipped without consumer wiring; this PR closes that loop.

## What lands

The credential-proxy + bridge that Phase 1a shipped get wired into the SDK runner's startup path:

| Slice | Change |
|---|---|
| `@ax/chat-orchestrator` | Per-`agent:invoke` proxy lifecycle: `proxy:open-session` after `agents:resolve`, before `sandbox:open-session` (carrying a `proxyConfig` blob). `proxy:close-session` in `finally` (I7). Soft-dep gated via `bus.hasService` so non-credential-proxy presets stay supported until Phase 5/6. |
| `@ax/sandbox-subprocess` | New optional `proxyConfig` field on `OpenSessionInput`. When set, writes the MITM CA PEM to a per-session tmpfile and injects `HTTPS_PROXY` / `HTTP_PROXY` / `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS` / `SSL_CERT_FILE` / `AX_PROXY_*` / `envMap` into the runner env. CA cleanup piggybacks on the existing tempdir rm. |
| `@ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner` | Reads `AX_PROXY_ENDPOINT` / `AX_PROXY_UNIX_SOCKET`; `AX_LLM_PROXY_URL` becomes optional XOR. Bridge mode (`AX_PROXY_UNIX_SOCKET`) starts `@ax/credential-proxy-bridge` and rewrites `HTTP(S)_PROXY` in-process. Direct mode (`AX_PROXY_ENDPOINT`) is a no-op — sandbox-subprocess set the env at spawn. Both modes drop `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` and call `api.anthropic.com` directly through `HTTPS_PROXY` (I8). The placeholder `ax-cred:<hex>` flows as `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`. |
| `@ax/cli` | Loads `@ax/credential-proxy` on `cfg.llm === 'anthropic'` (TCP loopback). Mock-LLM path keeps the legacy in-sandbox llm-proxy — loading the proxy in mock mode would force the SDK runner onto direct-egress and turn every canary into a real network call. |
| `@ax/cli/dev-agents-stub` | Default agent now returns `allowedHosts: ['api.anthropic.com']` and `requiredCredentials: { ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: { ref: 'anthropic-api', kind: 'api-key' } }`. Users seed the credential before the canary works: `ax-next credentials set anthropic-api`. |
| `@ax/preset-k8s` | Loads `@ax/credential-proxy` on a Unix socket (default `/var/run/ax/proxy.sock`, overridable via `K8sPresetConfig.credentialProxy.socketPath` + the `AX_PROXY_SOCKET_PATH` env var). |
| `@ax/audit-log` | Subscribes to `event.http-egress` and persists one row per egress (key `egress:<sessionId>:<timestamp>`). |

End-to-end coverage: a new gated test (`packages/cli/src/__tests__/credential-proxy.e2e.test.ts`) round-trips a real Anthropic call through the credential-proxy when `AX_TEST_ANTHROPIC_KEY` is set; CI skips automatically.

## Phase 1a half-wired window — CLOSED

Phase 1a (PR #1) shipped `@ax/credential-proxy` and `@ax/credential-proxy-bridge` without any consumer wiring. This PR closes that window:

- `@ax/cli` (`packages/cli/src/main.ts`) loads the proxy plugin on the `cfg.llm === 'anthropic'` branch.
- `@ax/preset-k8s` (`presets/k8s/src/index.ts`) loads the proxy plugin (Unix socket).
- `@ax/chat-orchestrator` calls `proxy:open-session` per `agent:invoke` and closes it in `finally` (I7).
- `@ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner` reads the proxy env, starts the bridge if needed (k8s mode), and redirects SDK calls through `HTTPS_PROXY` (drops `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` per I8).
- `@ax/audit-log` persists each `event.http-egress` fire.

Both Phase 1a packages are now reachable from the canary acceptance test in the CLI preset (and the gated real-API e2e). Half-wired window: closed.

## Boundary review — `OpenSessionInput.proxyConfig` (new field)

- **Alternate impl this hook could have:** a vault-backed proxy that exposes a different endpoint shape (e.g. `vault://<role>` URI). The `endpoint`/`unixSocketPath` split keeps the wire surface flexible without committing to a specific protocol — a third variant could land as `proxyConfig.endpoint = 'vault://...'` without renames.
- **Payload field names that might leak:** none. `endpoint` is a generic URI; `unixSocketPath` is a generic path; `caCertPem` is a generic standard format; `envMap` is a generic key-value map. No git/k8s/sqlite vocabulary.
- **Subscriber risk:** sandbox-subprocess is the only consumer today. Future k8s sandbox (Phase 7-9) will consume the same shape. No subscribers parse `endpoint` for protocol semantics — it's passed verbatim into `HTTPS_PROXY`.
- **Wire surface:** none. `OpenSessionInput` is in-process only; not exposed on the IPC bridge to sandboxes (the runner reads the resolved env, not the input shape).

## Invariants verified (from impl plan)

| | What | Where verified |
|---|---|---|
| I1 | Real credentials never enter sandbox process | sandbox-subprocess `open-session.test.ts` (env-injection test reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY = ax-cred:<hex> placeholder, NOT a real key); I9 audit on the runner side (placeholder forwarded, IPC bearer never sent upstream) |
| I2 | No cross-plugin imports | `pnpm lint` clean; orchestrator + sandbox-subprocess each define their own `ProxyConfig` shape |
| I3 | `proxyConfig` field names don't leak backend | Boundary review § (this file) |
| I4 | Boundary review recorded | this file's "Boundary review" section |
| I5 | Capabilities explicit | orchestrator uses `bus.hasService` runtime check (soft dep); manifest unchanged. sandbox-subprocess manifest unchanged (it receives the resolved blob — no proxy-hook calls) |
| I6 | Half-wired window closed | "Phase 1a half-wired window — CLOSED" § (this file); CLI canary loads both packages |
| I7 | `proxy:close-session` always fires | orchestrator test "calls proxy:close-session in finally even when sandbox:open-session throws" |
| I8 | SDK runner stops setting `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` when proxy is wired | `proxy-startup.test.ts` "direct mode: no ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL" + "bridge mode: no ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL" |
| I9 | `AX_LLM_PROXY_URL` optional XOR | `env.test.ts` covers all three modes (legacy, AX_PROXY_ENDPOINT only, AX_PROXY_UNIX_SOCKET only) + the all-missing failure |
| I10 | Bridge mode rewrites `HTTP(S)_PROXY` | `proxy-startup.test.ts` "bridge mode: starts the bridge, rewrites process.env.HTTPS_PROXY" |

## Out-of-scope (deferred)

These were called out as Phase 2 non-goals in the impl plan and remain so:

- **`proxy:rotate-session` plumbing.** Single-turn flow doesn't rotate; Phase 3 (OAuth) earns it.
- **K8s deployment manifests** (helm chart updates for proxy Unix socket + emptyDir mount). The preset wires the plugin; the chart needs a follow-up PR.
- **Native runner deletion.** `@ax/agent-native-runner` and `@ax/llm-proxy-anthropic-format` stay loaded for the native runner. Phase 5/6 deletes them.
- **`credentials:get` reshape to `(ref, { userId })`.** Still Phase 3 (OAuth) work; the proxy calls credentials with the current `{id} → {value}` shape.
- **Allowlist enforcement at the agents-resolve boundary in production.** The dev stub returns a hardcoded allowlist; the real `@ax/agents` plugin's `Agent` shape doesn't yet have `allowedHosts` / `requiredCredentials` columns. Phase 9.5+ extends real `@ax/agents`. In the meantime, k8s deploys with this PR see proxy:open-session called with empty allowlist + empty credentials → all egress blocked. **Documented operator action: seed at least one agent record with the right shape before running canary.**
- **Canary token integration.** Open question §7 in the design — defer until a concrete threat model justifies it.

## Stats

- 8 commits on the branch
- 7 packages touched + 1 preset
- ~12 new tests (orchestrator: 5; sandbox-subprocess: 4; runner: 5; CLI wiring: 1; e2e: 1 gated)
- ~190 LOC of new test code; ~280 LOC of new production code
- `pnpm build` clean
- `pnpm test` clean across all touched packages

## Follow-ups (don't block this PR)

- **Helm chart updates** for the credential-proxy Unix socket emptyDir mount on host + sandbox pods.
- **Real `@ax/agents` schema migration** to add `allowed_hosts text[]` and `required_credentials jsonb` columns + matching admin UI fields. Without this, k8s production deploys can't seed proxy-aware agents.
- **Phase 5/6** — delete `@ax/agent-native-runner` + `@ax/llm-proxy-anthropic-format` once the SDK runner is the only path. The legacy branches in `proxy-startup.ts` and `setupProxy()` go with them.
- **Phase 3** — OAuth credential refresh + the `credentials:get` reshape. `proxy:rotate-session` plumbing earns its weight here.

content.text: Now open the PR:

input.command:
gh pr create --title "Phase 2 — wire credential-proxy + bridge into the SDK runner" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

Closes the half-wired window opened by Phase 1a (PR #1). The credential-proxy + bridge now sit inside the `agent:invoke` lifecycle: orchestrator opens a per-session proxy, sandbox-subprocess threads the placeholder env + CA into the runner, and the SDK runner makes real Anthropic API calls with `ax-cred:` placeholder keys that the proxy substitutes mid-flight.

- Real credentials never enter the sandbox process (I1).
- Backend-agnostic boundary on `OpenSessionInput.proxyConfig` (I3) — TCP loopback for subprocess, Unix socket for k8s.
- Soft-dep gating via `bus.hasService` keeps non-credential-proxy presets working through Phase 5/6.
- `proxy:close-session` always fires once per `proxy:open-session` (I7).
- SDK runner stops setting `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` when proxy mode is on; calls `api.anthropic.com` directly through `HTTPS_PROXY` (I8).
- `AX_LLM_PROXY_URL` is now optional XOR with `AX_PROXY_*` (I9).
- `audit-log` subscribes to `event.http-egress` and persists one row per egress.

Full PR notes (boundary review, invariants table, half-wired window closure, follow-ups): `docs/plans/2026-04-28-phase-2-pr-notes.md`.

## Boundary review — `OpenSessionInput.proxyConfig` (new field)

- **Alternate impl:** vault-backed proxy (`vault://<role>` URI) — the `endpoint` / `unixSocketPath` split keeps the wire surface flexible without renames.
- **Leaky names:** none. `endpoint`, `unixSocketPath`, `caCertPem`, `envMap` are all backend-agnostic.
- **Subscriber risk:** sandbox-subprocess passes `endpoint` verbatim into `HTTPS_PROXY`; future k8s sandbox does the same.
- **Wire surface:** none. `OpenSessionInput` is in-process only; not exposed on the IPC bridge.

## Phase 1a half-wired window — CLOSED

- `@ax/cli` loads the proxy plugin (gated on `cfg.llm === 'anthropic'`).
- `@ax/preset-k8s` loads the proxy plugin (Unix socket).
- `@ax/chat-orchestrator` opens + closes the proxy per `agent:invoke`.
- `@ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner` reads proxy env + starts the bridge in k8s mode.
- `@ax/audit-log` persists every egress event.

Both Phase 1a packages are now reachable from the canary acceptance test.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm build` clean
- [x] `pnpm lint` clean
- [x] `pnpm --filter @ax/chat-orchestrator test` — 25 passed
- [x] `pnpm --filter @ax/sandbox-subprocess test` — 18 passed
- [x] `pnpm --filter @ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner test` — 80 passed
- [x] `pnpm --filter @ax/cli test` — 81 passed (1 skipped: gated real-API e2e)
- [x] `pnpm --filter @ax/audit-log test` — 6 passed
- [x] `pnpm --filter @ax/preset-k8s test` — 54 passed (incl. testcontainers)
- [ ] Local: `AX_TEST_ANTHROPIC_KEY=sk-ant-... pnpm --filter @ax/cli test -- credential-proxy.e2e` round-trips through real Anthropic upstream
- [ ] CI green

## Follow-ups (don't block this PR)

- Helm chart updates for the credential-proxy Unix socket emptyDir mount.
- `@ax/agents` schema migration: add `allowed_hosts text[]` + `required_credentials jsonb` columns. Until then, k8s production deploys block all egress because real `Agent` records lack the proxy fields. Operator must seed at least one agent record manually for the canary to round-trip.
- Phase 5/6 deletes `@ax/agent-native-runner` + `@ax/llm-proxy-anthropic-format`; the legacy branches in `proxy-startup.ts` go with them.
- Phase 3 (OAuth) earns `proxy:rotate-session` plumbing.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"

+# Phase 2 — PR notes
+
+**Branch:** `phase-2-runner-proxy-wiring`
+**Plan:** `docs/plans/2026-04-28-phase-2-runner-proxy-wiring-impl.md`
+**Design doc:** `docs/plans/2026-04-27-agent-centric-simplification-design.md` Section 1 + Section 5
+**Predecessor:** Phase 1a (PR #1) — credential-proxy + bridge shipped without consumer wiring; this PR closes that loop.
+
+## What lands
+
+The credential-proxy + bridge that Phase 1a shipped get wired into the SDK runner's startup path:
+
+| Slice | Change |
+|---|---|
+| `@ax/chat-orchestrator` | Per-`agent:invoke` proxy lifecycle: `proxy:open-session` after `agents:resolve`, before `sandbox:open-session` (carrying a `proxyConfig` blob). `proxy:close-session` in `finally` (I7). Soft-dep gated via `bus.hasService` so non-credential-proxy presets stay supported until Phase 5/6. |
+| `@ax/sandbox-subprocess` | New optional `proxyConfig` field on `OpenSessionInput`. When set, writes the MITM CA PEM to a per-session tmpfile and injects `HTTPS_PROXY` / `HTTP_PROXY` / `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS` / `SSL_CERT_FILE` / `AX_PROXY_*` / `envMap` into the runner env. CA cleanup piggybacks on the existing tempdir rm. |
+| `@ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner` | Reads `AX_PROXY_ENDPOINT` / `AX_PROXY_UNIX_SOCKET`; `AX_LLM_PROXY_URL` becomes optional XOR. Bridge mode (`AX_PROXY_UNIX_SOCKET`) starts `@ax/credential-proxy-bridge` and rewrites `HTTP(S)_PROXY` in-process. Direct mode (`AX_PROXY_ENDPOINT`) is a no-op — sandbox-subprocess set the env at spawn. Both modes drop `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` and call `api.anthropic.com` directly through `HTTPS_PROXY` (I8). The placeholder `ax-cred:<hex>` flows as `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`. |
+| `@ax/cli` | Loads `@ax/credential-proxy` on `cfg.llm === 'anthropic'` (TCP loopback). Mock-LLM path keeps the legacy in-sandbox llm-proxy — loading the proxy in mock mode would force the SDK runner onto direct-egress and turn every canary into a real network call. |
+| `@ax/cli/dev-agents-stub` | Default agent now returns `allowedHosts: ['api.anthropic.com']` and `requiredCredentials: { ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: { ref: 'anthropic-api', kind: 'api-key' } }`. Users seed the credential before the canary works: `ax-next credentials set anthropic-api`. |
+| `@ax/preset-k8s` | Loads `@ax/credential-proxy` on a Unix socket (default `/var/run/ax/proxy.sock`, overridable via `K8sPresetConfig.credentialProxy.socketPath` + the `AX_PROXY_SOCKET_PATH` env var). |
+| `@ax/audit-log` | Subscribes to `event.http-egress` and persists one row per egress (key `egress:<sessionId>:<timestamp>`). |
+
+End-to-end coverage: a new gated test (`packages/cli/src/__tests__/credential-proxy.e2e.test.ts`) round-trips a real Anthropic call through the credential-proxy when `AX_TEST_ANTHROPIC_KEY` is set; CI skips automatically.
+
+## Phase 1a half-wired window — CLOSED
+
+Phase 1a (PR #1) shipped `@ax/credential-proxy` and `@ax/credential-proxy-bridge` without any consumer wiring. This PR closes that window:
+
+- `@ax/cli` (`packages/cli/src/main.ts`) loads the proxy plugin on the `cfg.llm === 'anthropic'` branch.
+- `@ax/preset-k8s` (`presets/k8s/src/index.ts`) loads the proxy plugin (Unix socket).
+- `@ax/chat-orchestrator` calls `proxy:open-session` per `agent:invoke` and closes it in `finally` (I7).
+- `@ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner` reads the proxy env, starts the bridge if needed (k8s mode), and redirects SDK calls through `HTTPS_PROXY` (drops `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` per I8).
+- `@ax/audit-log` persists each `event.http-egress` fire.
+
+Both Phase 1a packages are now reachable from the canary acceptance test in the CLI preset (and the gated real-API e2e). Half-wired window: closed.
+
+## Boundary review — `OpenSessionInput.proxyConfig` (new field)
+
+- **Alternate impl this hook could have:** a vault-backed proxy that exposes a different endpoint shape (e.g. `vault://<role>` URI). The `endpoint`/`unixSocketPath` split keeps the wire surface flexible without committing to a specific protocol — a third variant could land as `proxyConfig.endpoint = 'vault://...'` without renames.
+- **Payload field names that might leak:** none. `endpoint` is a generic URI; `unixSocketPath` is a generic path; `caCertPem` is a generic standard format; `envMap` is a generic key-value map. No git/k8s/sqlite vocabulary.
+- **Subscriber risk:** sandbox-subprocess is the only consumer today. Future k8s sandbox (Phase 7-9) will consume the same shape. No subscribers parse `endpoint` for protocol semantics — it's passed verbatim into `HTTPS_PROXY`.
+- **Wire surface:** none. `OpenSessionInput` is in-process only; not exposed on the IPC bridge to sandboxes (the runner reads the resolved env, not the input shape).
+
+## Invariants verified (from impl plan)
+
+| | What | Where verified |
+|---|---|---|
+| I1 | Real credentials never enter sandbox process | sandbox-subprocess `open-session.test.ts` (env-injection test reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY = ax-cred:<hex> placeholder, NOT a real key); I9 audit on the runner side (placeholder forwarded, IPC bearer never sent upstream) |
+| I2 | No cross-plugin imports | `pnpm lint` clean; orchestrator + sandbox-subprocess each define their own `ProxyConfig` shape |
+| I3 | `proxyConfig` field names don't leak backend | Boundary review § (this file) |
+| I4 | Boundary review recorded | this file's "Boundary review" section |
+| I5 | Capabilities explicit | orchestrator uses `bus.hasService` runtime check (soft dep); manifest unchanged. sandbox-subprocess manifest unchanged (it receives the resolved blob — no proxy-hook calls) |
+| I6 | Half-wired window closed | "Phase 1a half-wired window — CLOSED" § (this file); CLI canary loads both packages |
+| I7 | `proxy:close-session` always fires | orchestrator test "calls proxy:close-session in finally even when sandbox:open-session throws" |
+| I8 | SDK runner stops setting `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` when proxy is wired | `proxy-startup.test.ts` "direct mode: no ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL" + "bridge mode: no ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL" |
+| I9 | `AX_LLM_PROXY_URL` optional XOR | `env.test.ts` covers all three modes (legacy, AX_PROXY_ENDPOINT only, AX_PROXY_UNIX_SOCKET only) + the all-missing failure |
+| I10 | Bridge mode rewrites `HTTP(S)_PROXY` | `proxy-startup.test.ts` "bridge mode: starts the bridge, rewrites process.env.HTTPS_PROXY" |
+
+## Out-of-scope (deferred)
+
+These were called out as Phase 2 non-goals in the impl plan and remain so:
+
+- **`proxy:rotate-session` plumbing.** Single-turn flow doesn't rotate; Phase 3 (OAuth) earns it.
+- **K8s deployment manifests** (helm chart updates for proxy Unix socket + emptyDir mount). The preset wires the plugin; the chart needs a follow-up PR.
+- **Native runner deletion.** `@ax/agent-native-runner` and `@ax/llm-proxy-anthropic-format` stay loaded for the native runner. Phase 5/6 deletes them.
+- **`credentials:get` reshape to `(ref, { userId })`.** Still Phase 3 (OAuth) work; the proxy calls credentials with the current `{id} → {value}` shape.
+- **Allowlist enforcement at the agents-resolve boundary in production.** The dev stub returns a hardcoded allowlist; the real `@ax/agents` plugin's `Agent` shape doesn't yet have `allowedHosts` / `requiredCredentials` columns. Phase 9.5+ extends real `@ax/agents`. In the meantime, k8s deploys with this PR see proxy:open-session called with empty allowlist + empty credentials → all egress blocked. **Documented operator action: seed at least one agent record with the right shape before running canary.**
+- **Canary token integration.** Open question §7 in the design — defer until a concrete threat model justifies it.
+
+## Stats
+
+- 8 commits on the branch
+- 7 packages touched + 1 preset
+- ~12 new tests (orchestrator: 5; sandbox-subprocess: 4; runner: 5; CLI wiring: 1; e2e: 1 gated)

# Phase 2 — PR notes

**Branch:** `phase-2-runner-proxy-wiring`
**Plan:** `docs/plans/2026-04-28-phase-2-runner-proxy-wiring-impl.md`
**Design doc:** `docs/plans/2026-04-27-agent-centric-simplification-design.md` Section 1 + Section 5
**Predecessor:** Phase 1a (PR #1) — credential-proxy + bridge shipped without consumer wiring; this PR closes that loop.

## What lands

The credential-proxy + bridge that Phase 1a shipped get wired into the SDK runner's startup path:

| Slice | Change |
|---|---|
| `@ax/chat-orchestrator` | Per-`agent:invoke` proxy lifecycle: `proxy:open-session` after `agents:resolve`, before `sandbox:open-session` (carrying a `proxyConfig` blob). `proxy:close-session` in `finally` (I7). Soft-dep gated via `bus.hasService` so non-credential-proxy presets stay supported until Phase 5/6. |
| `@ax/sandbox-subprocess` | New optional `proxyConfig` field on `OpenSessionInput`. When set, writes the MITM CA PEM to a per-session tmpfile and injects `HTTPS_PROXY` / `HTTP_PROXY` / `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS` / `SSL_CERT_FILE` / `AX_PROXY_*` / `envMap` into the runner env. CA cleanup piggybacks on the existing tempdir rm. |
| `@ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner` | Reads `AX_PROXY_ENDPOINT` / `AX_PROXY_UNIX_SOCKET`; `AX_LLM_PROXY_URL` becomes optional XOR. Bridge mode (`AX_PROXY_UNIX_SOCKET`) starts `@ax/credential-proxy-bridge` and rewrites `HTTP(S)_PROXY` in-process. Direct mode (`AX_PROXY_ENDPOINT`) is a no-op — sandbox-subprocess set the env at spawn. Both modes drop `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` and call `api.anthropic.com` directly through `HTTPS_PROXY` (I8). The placeholder `ax-cred:<hex>` flows as `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`. |
| `@ax/cli` | Loads `@ax/credential-proxy` on `cfg.llm === 'anthropic'` (TCP loopback). Mock-LLM path keeps the legacy in-sandbox llm-proxy — loading the proxy in mock mode would force the SDK runner onto direct-egress and turn every canary into a real network call. |
| `@ax/cli/dev-agents-stub` | Default agent now returns `allowedHosts: ['api.anthropic.com']` and `requiredCredentials: { ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: { ref: 'anthropic-api', kind: 'api-key' } }`. Users seed the credential before the canary works: `ax-next credentials set anthropic-api`. |
| `@ax/preset-k8s` | Loads `@ax/credential-proxy` on a Unix socket (default `/var/run/ax/proxy.sock`, overridable via `K8sPresetConfig.credentialProxy.socketPath` + the `AX_PROXY_SOCKET_PATH` env var). |
| `@ax/audit-log` | Subscribes to `event.http-egress` and persists one row per egress (key `egress:<sessionId>:<timestamp>`). |

End-to-end coverage: a new gated test (`packages/cli/src/__tests__/credential-proxy.e2e.test.ts`) round-trips a real Anthropic call through the credential-proxy when `AX_TEST_ANTHROPIC_KEY` is set; CI skips automatically.

## Phase 1a half-wired window — CLOSED

Phase 1a (PR #1) shipped `@ax/credential-proxy` and `@ax/credential-proxy-bridge` without any consumer wiring. This PR closes that window:

- `@ax/cli` (`packages/cli/src/main.ts`) loads the proxy plugin on the `cfg.llm === 'anthropic'` branch.
- `@ax/preset-k8s` (`presets/k8s/src/index.ts`) loads the proxy plugin (Unix socket).
- `@ax/chat-orchestrator` calls `proxy:open-session` per `agent:invoke` and closes it in `finally` (I7).
- `@ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner` reads the proxy env, starts the bridge if needed (k8s mode), and redirects SDK calls through `HTTPS_PROXY` (drops `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` per I8).
- `@ax/audit-log` persists each `event.http-egress` fire.

Both Phase 1a packages are now reachable from the canary acceptance test in the CLI preset (and the gated real-API e2e). Half-wired window: closed.

## Boundary review — `OpenSessionInput.proxyConfig` (new field)

- **Alternate impl this hook could have:** a vault-backed proxy that exposes a different endpoint shape (e.g. `vault://<role>` URI). The `endpoint`/`unixSocketPath` split keeps the wire surface flexible without committing to a specific protocol — a third variant could land as `proxyConfig.endpoint = 'vault://...'` without renames.
- **Payload field names that might leak:** none. `endpoint` is a generic URI; `unixSocketPath` is a generic path; `caCertPem` is a generic standard format; `envMap` is a generic key-value map. No git/k8s/sqlite vocabulary.
- **Subscriber risk:** sandbox-subprocess is the only consumer today. Future k8s sandbox (Phase 7-9) will consume the same shape. No subscribers parse `endpoint` for protocol semantics — it's passed verbatim into `HTTPS_PROXY`.
- **Wire surface:** none. `OpenSessionInput` is in-process only; not exposed on the IPC bridge to sandboxes (the runner reads the resolved env, not the input shape).

## Invariants verified (from impl plan)

| | What | Where verified |
|---|---|---|
| I1 | Real credentials never enter sandbox process | sandbox-subprocess `open-session.test.ts` (env-injection test reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY = ax-cred:<hex> placeholder, NOT a real key); I9 audit on the runner side (placeholder forwarded, IPC bearer never sent upstream) |
| I2 | No cross-plugin imports | `pnpm lint` clean; orchestrator + sandbox-subprocess each define their own `ProxyConfig` shape |
| I3 | `proxyConfig` field names don't leak backend | Boundary review § (this file) |
| I4 | Boundary review recorded | this file's "Boundary review" section |
| I5 | Capabilities explicit | orchestrator uses `bus.hasService` runtime check (soft dep); manifest unchanged. sandbox-subprocess manifest unchanged (it receives the resolved blob — no proxy-hook calls) |
| I6 | Half-wired window closed | "Phase 1a half-wired window — CLOSED" § (this file); CLI canary loads both packages |
| I7 | `proxy:close-session` always fires | orchestrator test "calls proxy:close-session in finally even when sandbox:open-session throws" |
| I8 | SDK runner stops setting `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` when proxy is wired | `proxy-startup.test.ts` "direct mode: no ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL" + "bridge mode: no ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL" |
| I9 | `AX_LLM_PROXY_URL` optional XOR | `env.test.ts` covers all three modes (legacy, AX_PROXY_ENDPOINT only, AX_PROXY_UNIX_SOCKET only) + the all-missing failure |
| I10 | Bridge mode rewrites `HTTP(S)_PROXY` | `proxy-startup.test.ts` "bridge mode: starts the bridge, rewrites process.env.HTTPS_PROXY" |

## Out-of-scope (deferred)

These were called out as Phase 2 non-goals in the impl plan and remain so:

- **`proxy:rotate-session` plumbing.** Single-turn flow doesn't rotate; Phase 3 (OAuth) earns it.
- **K8s deployment manifests** (helm chart updates for proxy Unix socket + emptyDir mount). The preset wires the plugin; the chart needs a follow-up PR.
- **Native runner deletion.** `@ax/agent-native-runner` and `@ax/llm-proxy-anthropic-format` stay loaded for the native runner. Phase 5/6 deletes them.
- **`credentials:get` reshape to `(ref, { userId })`.** Still Phase 3 (OAuth) work; the proxy calls credentials with the current `{id} → {value}` shape.
- **Allowlist enforcement at the agents-resolve boundary in production.** The dev stub returns a hardcoded allowlist; the real `@ax/agents` plugin's `Agent` shape doesn't yet have `allowedHosts` / `requiredCredentials` columns. Phase 9.5+ extends real `@ax/agents`. In the meantime, k8s deploys with this PR see proxy:open-session called with empty allowlist + empty credentials → all egress blocked. **Documented operator action: seed at least one agent record with the right shape before running canary.**
- **Canary token integration.** Open question §7 in the design — defer until a concrete threat model justifies it.

## Stats

- 8 commits on the branch
- 7 packages touched + 1 preset
- ~12 new tests (orchestrator: 5; sandbox-subprocess: 4; runner: 5; CLI wiring: 1; e2e: 1 gated)
- ~190 LOC of new test code; ~280 LOC of new production code
- `pnpm build` clean
- `pnpm test` clean across all touched packages

## Follow-ups (don't block this PR)

- **Helm chart updates** for the credential-proxy Unix socket emptyDir mount on host + sandbox pods.
- **Real `@ax/agents` schema migration** to add `allowed_hosts text[]` and `required_credentials jsonb` columns + matching admin UI fields. Without this, k8s production deploys can't seed proxy-aware agents.
- **Phase 5/6** — delete `@ax/agent-native-runner` + `@ax/llm-proxy-anthropic-format` once the SDK runner is the only path. The legacy branches in `proxy-startup.ts` and `setupProxy()` go with them.
- **Phase 3** — OAuth credential refresh + the `credentials:get` reshape. `proxy:rotate-session` plumbing earns its weight here.

input.command:
git add packages/chat-orchestrator && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(chat-orchestrator): pair proxy hooks + close session even on scheme error (CodeRabbit)

Two correctness bugs in the Phase 2 orchestrator wiring, both flagged
in PR review:

1. **Pair proxy:open-session and proxy:close-session before enabling
   proxy mode.** Previously gated only on proxy:open-session; a skewed
   preset that wired open without close would open per-session proxy
   state on every agent:invoke that could never be closed. We now
   require BOTH and surface a structured `proxy-hooks-misconfigured`
   terminated outcome when only one is registered.

2. **Mark proxyOpened=true BEFORE endpointToProxyConfig().** The
   helper throws on unrecognized scheme; the prior ordering left
   proxyOpened=false in that case, so the finally block skipped the
   close call and leaked the just-opened upstream session. The catch
   block now also fires close when proxyOpened is true so the
   terminated path is symmetric with the success path.

Tests cover both: skewed-preset rejection + bad-scheme cleanup.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

input.command:
git add packages/sandbox-subprocess && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(sandbox-subprocess): skip legacy llm-proxy + rollback on CA failure + redact echo-stub (CodeRabbit)

Three correctness/security fixes from PR review:

1. **Skip legacy llm-proxy:start when proxyConfig is set.**
   openSessionImpl previously started @ax/llm-proxy-anthropic-format
   unconditionally and injected AX_LLM_PROXY_URL even when the runner
   was on the credential-proxy direct-egress path and would never read
   it. That violates CLAUDE.md's half-wired-code policy AND every
   proxied session allocated an unused TCP listener. Now gated on
   useLegacyProxy = (input.proxyConfig === undefined); the close
   handler and spawn-failed rollback are gated on legacyProxyStarted
   so the new path doesn't try to stop something it never started.

2. **Rollback ipc + session + tempdir when CA write fails.** The
   fs.writeFile for the MITM CA cert runs after session:create and
   ipc:start. If it threw, the function exited without rollback,
   leaking a live token + bound listener. Same shape as the existing
   spawn-failed rollback. Surfaces as PluginError(code='ca-write-failed').

3. **Redact ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in the echo-stub fixture.** A real key
   set in process.env (over-eager test runner, dev's local secrets)
   would have echoed verbatim to stdout, which captured test logs
   may persist. Now: ax-cred:* placeholders pass through unchanged
   (the assertion shape Phase 2 tests need); anything else renders
   as '[redacted]'.

Tests cover (1) AX_LLM_PROXY_URL must be null when proxyConfig is
set, and (2) ipc:start can rebind the same sessionId after a
forced CA-write failure (proves the prior listener slot was freed).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
